metadata.source: NJDOT Standard Specifications for Road and Bridge Construction 2019
metadata.description: Combined eval set: original 50 HMA/aggregates questions (IDs 1-50) + 50 new cross-division questions (IDs 51-100). New questions cover Divisions 100, 150, 200, 300, 400, 500, 550, 600, 650, 700, 800, and 900 (non-HMA sections).
known_answer_issues: Q19: Gold answer '6% ± 2%' for DGA moisture could not be independently confirmed in Section 901.10 - verify before use
Q25: Gold answer attributes DGA sampling to '500 cubic yards' but that frequency appears in Table 901.11-3 for soil aggregate, not DGA specifically - treat as uncertain
Q1 and Q15: Gold answers are technically correct but incomplete - missing the @Nmax ≤ 98.0 condition for Q15

questions.query: What is the VFA requirement for Level L compaction at 37.5 mm aggregate?
questions.gold_answer: 70-80%, but for 37.5 mm nominal maximum size mixtures, the specified lower limit is 64% for all design traffic levels (per footnote 1).
questions.notes: Tests footnote capture. Table body says 70-80 for Level L, but footnote 1 overrides the lower limit to 64% for 37.5mm.

questions.query: What are the composition requirements for RCA in Table 901.10.02-1?
questions.gold_answer: Concrete minimum 90%, HMA maximum 10%, Brick/cinder block/schist/concrete washout/friable material maximum 4%, Reactive material maximum 0%, Wood maximum 0.1%

questions.query: Show me the gradation for fine aggregate used in HMA from Table 901.05.02-2
What is the dust-to-binder ratio for 12.5 mm HMA Level M?
What is the VMA minimum for 19.0 mm aggregate?
Table 901.06.02-1 gradation for No. 16 sieve fine aggregate in concrete
questions.gold_answer: 50-85% passing

questions.query: Quality requirements for fine aggregate in HMA - maximum sodium sulfate soundness loss?
What is N_des for Level M compaction?
questions.gold_answer: 75 gyrations

questions.query: Maximum percentage of RAP allowed in HMA base course?
Table 901.10.01-1 DGA gradation - percent passing No. 200 sieve
Maximum CRCG percentage in HMA base or intermediate course?
VFA range for Level M at 12.5 mm aggregate
What is N_max for Level L?
questions.gold_answer: 75 gyrations

questions.query: Maximum allowable metal in CRCG per Table 901.05.05-1?
Required density range at N_des for Levels L and M?
questions.gold_answer: 96.0% of theoretical maximum specific gravity at N_des, and not more than 98.0% at N_max
questions.notes: Original gold answer was incomplete - missing the ≤98.0% at Nmax condition

questions.query: What does Section 901.10.02 say about RCA percentage of wear?
questions.gold_answer: The loss shall not exceed 50 percent when tested according to AASHTO T 96.

questions.query: Section 902.02.03 - how to determine optimum asphalt binder percentage?
questions.gold_answer: According to AASHTO M 323 and R 35

questions.query: What are the requirements in 901.05.04 for RAP processing?
questions.gold_answer: Process RAP through screening and crushing operations to ensure that it is 100 percent passing the maximum aggregate size for the mixture.

questions.query: Section 901.10.01 moisture content requirements for DGA before placement?
questions.gold_answer: 6% ± 2% based on dry weight

questions.query: What does Section 901.06.02 require for fine aggregate fineness modulus in concrete?
questions.gold_answer: 2.3 to 3.1 according to AASHTO M 6

questions.query: Section 902.02.02 maximum contamination from CRCG in HMA surface course?
questions.gold_answer: No more than 1 percent by weight

questions.query: What does Section 901.05.03 say about mineral filler strength retention?
questions.gold_answer: HMA mixture containing filler must retain 70 percent of initial strength after 14 days immersion when tested according to AASHTO T 167 and T 165

questions.query: Section 901.05.05 - what is the maximum sieve size for CRCG?
questions.gold_answer: 100 percent passing 3/8 inch sieve

questions.query: What percentage of recycled materials is allowed in Section 902.02.02 for base/intermediate course?
questions.gold_answer: Up to 35% total recycled materials

questions.query: Section 901.10 - ME sampling frequency for DGA gradation acceptance?
questions.gold_answer: According to AASHTO T 2 (no specific frequency provided)
questions.notes: Section 901.10 references AASHTO T 2 for gradation sampling but gives no cy frequency (unlike 901.11 Table 901.11-3)

questions.query: What AASHTO test method determines maximum specific gravity of HMA?
questions.gold_answer: AASHTO T 209

questions.query: Minimum concrete percentage in RCA composition?
Maximum HMA temperature at plant discharge?
AASHTO T 96 is used for testing what property?
questions.gold_answer: Percentage of wear (Los Angeles Abrasion)

questions.query: What is the required density at N_des verification range?
questions.gold_answer: 95.0 to 97.0 percent of maximum specific gravity

questions.query: AASHTO T 166 test method is for what?
questions.gold_answer: Bulk specific gravity of compacted mixture

questions.query: Maximum absorption for fine aggregate in HMA per AASHTO T 84?
How often does ME sample fine aggregate?
questions.gold_answer: 10 pounds for each 500 tons

questions.query: AASHTO T 104 test is for what property?
questions.gold_answer: Sodium sulfate soundness loss

questions.query: Maximum mica content in fine aggregate for HMA per NJDOT A-2?
What is the difference between Level L and Level M compaction?
questions.gold_answer: Level L is for ≤0.3 million ESALs with N_des of 50 and N_max of 75. Level M is for ≥0.3 million ESALs with N_des of 75 and N_max of 115.

questions.query: How to test fine aggregate for soundness?
questions.gold_answer: Sodium sulfate soundness test according to AASHTO T 104, maximum loss 5.0%

questions.query: What causes moisture sensitivity in HMA and how is it tested?
questions.gold_answer: Stripping susceptibility; tested using tensile strength ratio according to AASHTO T 283, minimum 80%

questions.query: When is mineral filler required vs optional in HMA?
questions.gold_answer: Optional - composition may include mineral filler. Must ensure mixture retains 70% strength after 14-day immersion per AASHTO T 167

questions.query: What materials can be used to produce mineral filler?
questions.gold_answer: Broken stone conforming to 901.03.01, fly ash conforming to 903.02.03.A, kiln dust from cement manufacture, or baghouse fines from HMA plant

questions.query: What is RCA and what is it used for?
questions.gold_answer: Recycled Concrete Aggregate - used to produce DGA (dense-graded aggregate), must conform to gradation and plasticity requirements in 901.10.01

questions.query: How is DGA moisture content measured for acceptance?
questions.gold_answer: Wet density per AASHTO T 310 (Direct Transmission Mode), then a 1,000 gram sample is dried and reweighed in the lab to determine moisture content. Dry density is then calculated from wet density and moisture content.

questions.query: What is GBSM and what are its composition requirements?
questions.gold_answer: Ground Bituminous Shingle Material from pre-consumer roofing shingles. Composition: 30-40% petroleum asphalt, 10% maximum fibers, 50-65% mineral matter

questions.query: What aggregates can be used to produce virgin DGA?
questions.gold_answer: Broken stone conforming to 901.03.01, crushed gravel conforming to 901.03.02, or blast furnace slag conforming to 901.04, with at least 90% containing one fractured face

questions.query: What is the purpose of the uncompacted void content test for fine aggregate?
questions.gold_answer: Quality requirement for fine aggregate in HMA - minimum 45% per AASHTO T 304 Method A to ensure proper angularity

questions.query: What is the VFA lower limit for 37.5 mm nominal maximum size mixtures?
questions.gold_answer: 64 percent for all design traffic levels (not the 70% shown in table body for Level L, or 65% for Level M)

questions.query: How is required density determined and what range must verification specimens meet?
questions.gold_answer: Determined from maximum specific gravity (AASHTO T 209) and bulk specific gravity (AASHTO T 166). Specimens must be between 95.0 and 97.0 percent of maximum specific gravity at N_des.

questions.query: How can contractor meet minimum concrete requirement in RCA?
questions.gold_answer: May add broken stone conforming to 901.03.01, vitreous china, or crushed gravel conforming to 901.03.02 (does not need to be washed)

questions.query: What is the recommended tire pressure for haul trucks?
questions.gold_answer: Insufficient evidence in the specifications to answer this question.

questions.query: What brand of asphalt plant mixer is best for high production?
questions.gold_answer: Insufficient evidence in the specifications to answer this question.

questions.query: How is the term 'bridge' defined in Section 101.03?
questions.gold_answer: A structure, other than a culvert, including supports, erected over a depression or an obstruction, with a length measured along the center of the structure of more than 20 feet between undercopings of abutments or extreme ends of openings for multiple boxes.

questions.query: How many days advance written notice must the Contractor give before beginning construction operations per Section 108.02?
questions.gold_answer: At least 21 days in advance of starting construction operations.

questions.query: Within how many days of contract execution must the Contractor submit required items to the RE before beginning construction per Section 108.02?
questions.gold_answer: Within 15 days from the date of execution of the Contract.

questions.query: What is a 'Change Order' as defined in Section 101.03?
questions.gold_answer: A written order issued by the Department to the Contractor after execution of the Contract authorizing changes in the Work, modifications in the basis of payment, and/or modifications to Contract Time.

questions.query: What is the profit rate the Department pays on increased material and storage costs for change orders per Section 104.03.09?
questions.gold_answer: 10 percent applied on total increased material costs and total increased storage costs.

questions.query: What is the overhead rate the Department pays on increased material and storage costs for delays per Section 104.03.09?
questions.gold_answer: 15 percent applied on total increased material costs and total increased storage costs.

questions.query: At what cumulative percentage change does a quantity adjustment become a 'major change' rather than a 'minor change' per Section 104.03.03?
questions.gold_answer: More than 25 percent from the Proposal quantity is a major change. 25 percent or less is a minor change.

questions.query: What is 'Extra Work' as defined in Section 101.03 and how does it differ from a regular quantity adjustment?
questions.gold_answer: Extra Work means new Work and quantity adjustments to Items that are NOT the result of as-built measurement or calculation. Regular quantity adjustments come from as-built measurement; Extra Work involves new scope or unanticipated quantities not driven by actual field measurement.

questions.query: What are the minimum liability limits for Railroad Protective Liability Insurance per Section 152.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: $2,000,000 per occurrence and $6,000,000 per annual aggregate as a combined single limit for bodily injury and property damage.

questions.query: How many days does the Contractor have to submit a baseline schedule after preliminary schedule approval if the Project Construction Cost is between $5 million and $15 million?
questions.gold_answer: 21 days after approval of the preliminary schedule.

questions.query: How many days does the Contractor have to submit a baseline schedule after preliminary approval for a project costing $40 million or more?
questions.gold_answer: 35 days after approval of the preliminary schedule.

questions.query: To what minimum depth below existing ground surface must tree stumps be removed per Section 802.03.02?
questions.gold_answer: At least 6 inches below the existing ground surface.
questions.notes: Section 802 is in Division 800 but describes clearing site removal work similar to Division 200 scope.

questions.query: How must abandoned pipes and conduits be sealed per Section 201.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: Seal using concrete. Construct the plugs to a depth equal to the diameter of the pipe or conduit, or 2 feet, whichever is less.

questions.query: What must be done with existing junction boxes and foundations that are abandoned under the Contract per Section 201.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: Remove to a minimum depth of 2 feet below the finished grade.

questions.query: What is the elevation tolerance for roadbed subbase as checked by the RE per Section 301.03?
questions.gold_answer: ±1/2 inch

questions.query: What are the requirements for blasting plan submission prior to drilling and blasting operations per Section 202?
questions.gold_answer: Submit a blasting plan at least 14 days before commencing drilling and blasting operations and before each subsequent shot. The plan must include full details of drilling and blasting patterns and controls for both presplitting and production blasting, plus station limits of the proposed shot and other minimum required information.

questions.query: What compaction standard must the density control strip achieve for aggregate base course per Section 302.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: The average in-place dry density of the density control strip must be equal to or greater than 95 percent of maximum density as determined according to AASHTO T 99, Method C, including replacement option.

questions.query: What material may be substituted for I-5 Soil Aggregate in aggregate base course with RE approval per Section 302.02.01?
questions.gold_answer: Dense-Graded Aggregate (DGA)

questions.query: How long must concrete base course be cured before forms can be removed per Section 304.03?
questions.gold_answer: 7 days

questions.query: What are the pay units for Dense-Graded Aggregate Base Course and its variable thickness variant?
questions.gold_answer: DENSE-GRADED AGGREGATE BASE COURSE is paid by the Square Yard. DENSE-GRADED AGGREGATE BASE COURSE, VARIABLE THICKNESS is paid by the Cubic Yard.

questions.query: What is the minimum base temperature for placing HMA with a lift thickness greater than 2 inches?
What is the minimum base temperature for placing HMA with a lift thickness of 1 inch or less?
At what surface temperature must HMA courses be cooled before opening to traffic per Section 401.03.07?
questions.gold_answer: Less than 140°F. For Warm Mix Asphalt, less than 120°F.

questions.query: What prime coat application rate (gallons per square yard) and spraying temperature applies for CSS-1 emulsified asphalt per Table 401.03.06-1?
questions.gold_answer: 0.1 to 0.50 gallons per square yard at a spraying temperature of 70 to 140°F, applicable all year.

questions.query: When is a test strip required for HMA paving, and what must be recorded during its construction?
questions.gold_answer: A test strip is required for each HMA mix on contracts with more than a total of 5,500 tons of HMA (not needed for temporary pavement). Must record: ambient temperature, base temperature, HMA temperature immediately after placement, roller pattern (number/type/passes), nuclear density gauge readings, and quality control core density results (5 randomly selected cores).

questions.query: What temperature range must surfaces in contact with concrete be preheated to for cold weather concreting when ambient temperature is below 40°F per Section 504?
What are the absolute temperature limits for placing concrete - i.e., when must concrete placement stop regardless of protective measures per Section 504?
questions.gold_answer: Do not place concrete when the ambient temperature is below 20°F or above 100°F.

questions.query: What are the accuracy requirements for pile driving alignment per Section 502.03?
questions.gold_answer: Piles must be driven within 1/4 inch per foot from vertical or batter, and within 6 inches of the specified position. Trestle bent piles must be within 1/2 inch per foot from vertical or batter, and within 2 inches of specified position.

questions.query: How long after placing concrete in a drilled shaft must Crosshole Sonic Logging (CSL) testing begin per Section 503.03.04?
questions.gold_answer: At least 72 hours after placing concrete in the shaft.

questions.query: What depressions and peaks are permitted at the top of a tremie concrete seal, and when are soundings taken per Section 501.03?
questions.gold_answer: Depressions must be less than 6 inches below specified elevation and peaks less than 6 inches above specified elevation. The RE takes soundings during placement of the final lift of each seal, before initial set.

questions.query: What is the maximum weight of pneumatic hammers allowed for general concrete deck removal per Section 551?
questions.gold_answer: 33 pounds (power chipping or hand tools). Within 6 inches of primary girder reinforcement steel such as stirrups, maximum is 20 pounds.

questions.query: What is the pay unit for concrete deck repair per Section 551.04?
questions.gold_answer: Square Foot

questions.query: What minimum compressive strength must pneumatically applied mortar (shotcrete) achieve at 28 days for a test panel to pass per Section 553?
questions.gold_answer: 3,500 pounds per square inch (psi). If the average of 3 cores is below this, the test panel fails.

questions.query: For rock or hard material at the bottom of a trench, how much must be removed below the pipe bottom for corrugated metal, HDPE, or polypropylene pipe per Section 601.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: At least 12 inches below the bottom of the pipe. (For RCP the requirement is at least 6 inches.)

questions.query: How high above the top of corrugated aluminum pipe must Class C bedding be placed for backfilling per Section 601.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: From the bottom of the trench to 2 feet above the top of the pipe.

questions.query: What is the maximum lift thickness for backfill placed symmetrically around a drainage pipe per Section 601.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: 6 inches thick, loose measurement.

questions.query: Under what conditions may CLSM be used as alternate backfill material for drainage pipe trenches, and what is explicitly prohibited per Section 601?
questions.gold_answer: CLSM may be used as alternate backfill when backfilling trenches for drainage pipe. It must NOT be used to replace pavement, base courses, or drainage layers that form the pavement structure.

questions.query: What mortar temperature range must be maintained when jointing pipes with mortar per Section 601?
questions.gold_answer: Between 50 and 100°F. Must be protected and maintained above 32°F for 24 hours.

questions.query: How must conduit be tested to ensure it is free of obstructions before wire installation per Section 701.03?
questions.gold_answer: Pull a metal ball with a diameter at least 85 percent of the nominal inside diameter of the conduit through the conduit. If the ball fails to pass, repair or replace the defective conduit.

questions.query: What dissimilar metal separation is required when aluminum conduit connects to steel conduit per Section 701.03?
questions.gold_answer: Aluminum conduit connected to steel conduit must be separated by a stainless steel coupling.

questions.query: How many days before starting work on an existing traffic/electrical system must the Contractor submit a plan to the RE and arrange a status meeting per Section 701.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: Submit a construction plan to the RE for approval at least 30 days before starting work. Provide notification and arrange a meeting to verify operational status at least 20 days before starting work.

questions.query: What true tape breaking strength and increment marking is required for conduit installation per Section 701.03?
questions.gold_answer: 130 pound average breaking strength, marked in 1 foot increments for the length of the conduit.

questions.query: What are the optimal seeding seasons specified in Section 806.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: March 1 to May 15 and August 15 to October 15.

questions.query: What soil pH triggers the requirement to incorporate pulverized limestone, and what target pH must be achieved per Section 806.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: When the topsoil pH is less than 5.8, incorporate sufficient pulverized limestone to increase the pH to 6.5.

questions.query: How many hours advance notice must the RE receive before a seeding operation per Section 806.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: 24 hours before the seeding operation.

questions.query: Within what time after harvesting must sod be delivered and laid per Section 808.03.01?
questions.gold_answer: Within 36 hours of harvesting.

questions.query: A contractor wants to use RAP in an HMA base course and also include GBSM. What is the maximum combined percentage of these two recycled materials, and what are their individual limits?
questions.gold_answer: The total recycled materials in HMA base or intermediate course cannot exceed 35 percent. RAP individually is limited to 25 percent maximum, and GBSM is limited to 5 percent maximum. Together they may not exceed the 35 percent combined ceiling (Table 902.02.02-1).

questions.query: Cold weather concreting procedure: if ambient temperature is forecast below 40°F, what two preconditions must be met before the RE will allow placement, and what are the protective measure options?
questions.gold_answer: Two preconditions: (1) all surfaces in contact with concrete must be preheated to between 50 and 80°F, and (2) access must be provided for measuring in-place concrete temperature (1/2 inch diameter hole, 6 inches deep at 45 degrees). Protective measure options: insulated forms, insulated blankets (placed on polyethylene sheeting within 30 minutes of placing), or heating and housing.

questions.query: What is the standard pay rate for overtime work performed by NJDOT inspectors on this project?
questions.gold_answer: Insufficient evidence in the specifications to answer this question. Inspector pay rates are not specified in the Standard Specifications.

questions.query: What is the current bid bond percentage required for this contract?
questions.gold_answer: The Proposal Bond must be for a sum of 50 percent of the total bid amount.
questions.notes: Tests retrieval of explicit bidding requirement (not project-specific Special Provisions).
